market_summary.narrative: U.S. equities suffered a fourth consecutive week of losses in the week ending March 20, 2026, as an escalating conflict in the Middle East — centered on the U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran — sent Brent crude surging above $110/barrel, stoking stagflation fears and pushing Treasury yields higher. The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% at its March meeting, as Chair Powell dismissed stagflation concerns but acknowledged persistent inflation; market expectations for any 2026 rate cut collapsed to below 20%, with a small but rising probability (8–10%) of a hike by year-end. The S&P 500 closed at 6,506 (down nearly 6% from its February peak), while the tech-heavy Nasdaq led declines at -2.1% on the week and small-caps (Russell 2000) have shed over 8% in the past 30 days, with the only outperformers being energy and select defense-adjacent names.

value_picks.name: NIKE, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Nike's stock collapsed 34.7% from its 52-week high and sits at a multi-year low as the market penalizes a near-term earnings contraction—FY2025 revenue fell 9.8% amid a deliberate pull-back from undifferentiated wholesale channels and an aggressive inventory reset under CEO Elliott Hill. Tariff uncertainty on Asian manufacturing and a weak Greater China sales trajectory have compounded selling pressure in 2026. Critically, the market is pricing these as permanent impairments rather than the transient restructuring costs they represent, with trailing EPS depressed to $1.71 from a normalized $3.76 just two years ago. Insiders stepped up purchases in December 2025, signaling management conviction in the recovery path.
value_picks.bull_case: Nike's brand moat—the most recognized sportswear label globally—remains intact, and Elliott Hill's 'sport-first' strategy is already showing early signs of wholesale recovery in North America, with Dick's Sporting Goods channel checks pointing to strong sell-through on new running franchises. As the restructuring cycle bottoms in FY2027, consensus expects EPS to recover toward $3.50+, implying a normalized P/E below 15x at current prices—among the cheapest entry points in the brand's 30-year public history.

value_picks.name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
value_picks.why_undervalued: UnitedHealth is down 54.6% from its 52-week high following a perfect storm: a DOJ antitrust investigation into Medicare Advantage billing practices, the murder of CEO Brian Thompson that triggered intense sector-wide regulatory scrutiny, disappointing Q4 2025 earnings with a 40.9% EPS collapse, and a worse-than-expected 2026 revenue guidance of $439B that initially spooked investors. The market is treating these shocks as permanent impairments to the core insurance franchise rather than cyclical margin pressure at Optum, which generated $1 billion in AI-driven cost savings in 2025. At $275, UNH trades at just 20.8x trailing earnings—its cheapest valuation since 2014—despite running one of the most cash-generative healthcare businesses on earth, with $16.1B in TTM free cash flow.
value_picks.bull_case: With adjusted EPS guided to grow 8.6%+ in 2026 toward $17.85, UNH's earnings engine is already recovering and the market has yet to re-rate the stock from 'damaged insurer' to 'AI-powered health platform.' Viking Global initiated a fresh 1.2 million share position as of December 31, 2025, and Morningstar estimates the stock is 66% below its intrinsic fair value. The Optum segment—health services, pharmacy benefit management, and analytics—would be valued at $300B+ as a standalone, suggesting the core insurance business is being given away for free.

value_picks.name: Comcast Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Comcast has re-rated from a 9x P/E two years ago to a remarkable 5.4x today—the lowest among major US large-caps—as broadband subscriber losses to fixed wireless access from Verizon and T-Mobile have triggered a de-rating of the cable business model. The market fears a secular decline in residential broadband and continued cord-cutting eating into cable TV revenue. However, this view ignores that Comcast generates an extraordinary $21.9B in annual free cash flow—a 20.7% FCF yield—from a business mix that increasingly relies on business services broadband (still growing), Peacock's accelerating subscriber base, and Universal theme parks which delivered record attendance in 2025. Multiple DCF analyses peg intrinsic value between $75 and $82 per share, suggesting 60%+ undervaluation on even conservative assumptions.
value_picks.bull_case: Comcast's FCF yield of 20.7% is one of the highest of any investment-grade large-cap in the US market, and management's commitment to returning capital via buybacks and a growing dividend creates a powerful floor. The spin-off of NBCUniversal cable networks into 'SpinCo' announced in late 2025 would crystallize hidden value and allow the market to re-rate the remaining broadband and streaming businesses at higher multiples; Peacock has reached profitability ahead of schedule and adds a high-margin growth vector that the market largely ignores.

value_picks.name: The Home Depot, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Home Depot has fallen to its 52-week low as the US housing market remains in a historically deep freeze—Redfin data show housing turnover at a 30-year low in 2025, with 80% of existing mortgages locked in below current rates, suppressing renovation spending and large-ticket project activity. The market treats this as a structural decline in Home Depot's addressable market, but the company delivered Q4 FY2025 earnings that beat consensus by 20 cents per share and raised its quarterly dividend, demonstrating that the operational engine remains fully intact even in the trough. Tariff concerns around imported building materials have added another layer of fear in early 2026, yet HD's price points and scale give it better ability than smaller competitors to absorb or pass through cost increases.
value_picks.bull_case: Home Depot commands an irreplaceable two-player duopoly in US home improvement retail with $164.7B in revenue and a structural tailwind from the nation's severe 5-million-unit housing shortage—deferred repairs and eventual market normalization represent a multi-year earnings recovery. The company's accelerating push into Complex Pro (large contractor accounts) is a high-margin, recurring revenue stream that reduces cyclicality; as mortgage rates normalize and housing turnover recovers toward 3-4%, comparable store sales could accelerate toward high-single digits, validating consensus analyst targets of $421+ per share.

value_picks.name: McCormick & Company, Incorporated
value_picks.why_undervalued: McCormick has tumbled 36% from its 52-week high to a multi-year low as a combination of tariff-driven raw material cost pressure, a slight Q1 FY2026 EPS miss ($0.86 vs $0.87 expected), and an analyst downgrade to strong sell have triggered algorithmic selling into an already weak macro backdrop for consumer staples. The market is implicitly pricing permanent margin compression, yet McCormick's pricing power in the spice and flavor categories—where it holds an estimated 20%+ global market share with irreplaceable distribution and culinary trust—has consistently allowed it to recover margins within two years of every commodity cost cycle over the past three decades. The company's FY2026 EPS guidance of $3.05–$3.13 is essentially in-line with consensus, and its consumer division is growing volume, suggesting the fear is primarily macro- rather than company-specific.
value_picks.bull_case: McCormick sits at the intersection of two secular tailwinds: consumer premiumization of food flavor (especially in emerging markets) and a massive flavor manufacturing/industrial ingredients business that grows as global food companies reformulate products. With a debt-to-equity ratio of just 0.70x, a 2.7% dividend yield, and $740M in annual FCF that has grown every year for a decade, the stock offers a quality compounder at a price not seen since 2019 when it earned less than $2.50 per share. Bernstein's $93 price target represents 75% upside and reflects a return to historical EV/EBITDA multiples of 19-20x that the business has commanded in normal market conditions.

momentum_picks.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Micron reported fiscal Q2 2026 results on March 18 that shattered expectations across every metric: revenue of $23.86B nearly tripled year-over-year (vs. $8.05B in Q2 FY2025) and non-GAAP EPS of $12.20 beat the $9.31 consensus estimate by 31%. Management guided Q3 FY2026 revenue to $33.5B (±$750M) — a staggering 38% sequential jump well above the $24.3B analyst consensus — with non-GAAP gross margin guided to ~81%. The AI memory supercycle is the driver: Micron has begun volume shipments of HBM4 for Nvidia's Vera Rubin platform, data-center NAND revenue more than doubled sequentially, and management stated AI will drive DRAM and NAND bit TAM above 50% of industry TAM for the first time in 2026. Nine of ten sell-side analysts raised price targets post-earnings (consensus target ~$530), with Barclays setting a $675 target.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: The risk/reward at $422.90 is skewed to the upside but not without near-term caution. The stock sold off ~10% from its $471 all-time high on earnings despite blowout results, dragged lower by a capex guidance increase to above $25B for FY2026 — a >$5B raise that spooked investors worried about peak-cycle dynamics. The key risk is a demand air pocket if hyperscaler AI capex pauses or Samsung/SK Hynix aggressively expand HBM capacity ahead of schedule. At 20x trailing P/E with revenue growing triple digits and management guiding to 81% gross margins, the stock remains attractively valued relative to its earnings power — a $530 consensus target implies ~25% upside. For a fundamental buyer, the post-earnings dip to $423 represents one of the more compelling entries in the AI memory trade.

momentum_picks.name: Sable Offshore Corp.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Sable Offshore's long-dormant Santa Ynez Unit (SYU) — an offshore California oil complex shut since a 2015 pipeline rupture — received a federal order under the Defense Production Act (DPA) on March 13, 2026, overriding California state-level blockades driven by the Trump Administration's urgent need to offset Middle East supply disruptions. The company resumed oil flow on March 16 with an expected gross production rate of 50,000 barrels per day and projects first oil sales by April 1, 2026. This transforms SOC from a binary regulatory option play into an operating oil company: at 50,000 bbl/d with oil prices above $90/bbl, annual EBITDA could reach $600M–$800M against a current market cap of ~$1.6B, implying a near-trough multiple at first oil.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: The risk/reward is highly asymmetric but binary: the principal risk remains California's legal challenge to the DPA override, which could trigger injunctions and delay or halt production ramp before meaningful cash flows materialize. Additional operational risk includes the physical integrity of the decades-old pipeline and the 10–12 week lag to reach full 50K bbl/d throughput. On the reward side, once first oil is confirmed and production ramp validates the federal override, consensus targets of $20 represent 23% upside from current levels — and some bull cases project $30+ if the asset is eventually monetized through M&A. A long position sized appropriately for a binary event is appropriate for high-conviction fundamental buyers; the March profit-taking to $16.28 offers a better entry than the $17.14 peak.

momentum_picks.name: Hims & Hers Health, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Hims & Hers stock surged over 40% on March 9 following a landmark partnership announcement with Novo Nordisk to distribute FDA-approved GLP-1 medications — including Ozempic and Wegovy — directly through its telehealth platform. The deal resolved a high-profile legal dispute between the companies and transformed HIMS from a regulatory liability (compounding gray-market pharmacy) into an authorized distribution partner for the world's #1 obesity drug franchise. Multiple analysts upgraded the stock in the days following: Citigroup upgraded from Sell to Neutral, Needham upgraded to Buy with a $30 target, and Barclays raised its target to $29. Revenue grew +28.4% YoY to $617.8M in Q4 2025, and management has guided Q1 2026 to $600–$625M.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: The risk/reward is improving but balanced. The primary downside risk is that Q1 2026 guidance of $600–$625M came in below the $653M analyst consensus, suggesting execution headwinds even with the Novo deal in place. The stock's 3-month return of -38% (from December's $35 level) reflects how sharply sentiment shifted after the FDA banned compounded GLP-1s and prior litigation — overhang that is now partially resolved. At $22, HIMS trades at 43x trailing P/E and carries $1B in convertible debt, limiting downside protection. The bull case at $29–$30 (analyst targets) represents 32–36% upside and assumes the Novo distribution channel accelerates revenue towards $3B+ annually by 2027–2028 as GLP-1 penetration deepens. The stock is best suited for investors with tolerance for volatility and a multi-quarter time horizon on the GLP-1 ramp.

momentum_picks.name: GigaCloud Technology Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: GigaCloud Technology delivered a Q4 2025 earnings beat that turned heads across fundamental and quant desks: EPS of $1.04–$1.16 (sources slightly vary) crushed the $0.65–$0.77 consensus estimate by 50–60%, while Q4 revenue of $362.7M grew +22.6% YoY. The B2B large-parcel e-commerce marketplace is firing on all cylinders — Europe revenue grew 68% YoY, trailing-12-month GMV reached nearly $1.6B, and the 3P buyer base grew 17% to over 12,000 buyers. Management guided Q1 2026 revenue to $330–$355M (+21%+ YoY), ahead of consensus, and named TIME's America's Growth Leaders 2026. Crucially, the stock trades at just 11x forward earnings — a steep discount to the S&P 500 (22x) and technology sector peers (24x) — making it one of the most compelling value/growth combinations in the market.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: At 11x forward P/E with 20%+ revenue growth and consistent earnings beats, GCT offers one of the most attractive risk/reward profiles among small-cap technology names. The bear risks are real and specific: ocean freight rates are low and volatile (crimping service margins), CEO Lei Wu sold ~160,000 shares ($7M) in early March near the $42–$45 range, and the company faces integration risk from its New Classic acquisition. Analyst consensus sits at Moderate Buy but with average price targets that trail the current price — indicating sell-side coverage has not yet repriced for the 700% three-year run. The key upside scenario is that the company's European expansion and marketplace flywheel continue to compound at 20%+ with EPS approaching $5–$6 in FY2027, justifying a 15–18x multiple and a $75–$90 stock. For patient fundamental buyers, the pullback from the $48 ATH to $40 is a constructive re-entry.

momentum_picks.name: BWX Technologies, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: BWX Technologies is the dominant pure-play on nuclear power's structural renaissance, and 2026 is seeing multiple catalysts converge. The company's Q4 2025 revenue of $885.8M (+18.7% YoY) and full-year 2025 revenue of $3.1B (+18% vs 2024) demonstrated durable execution in its U.S. naval nuclear propulsion contracts and growing commercial nuclear services. TD Cowen initiated coverage with a Buy rating this week, highlighting BWXT's potential role in domestic uranium enrichment — reinforced by the company's January 2026 opening of its Centrifuge Manufacturing Development Facility in Oak Ridge, Tennessee. BWXT's stock is up 104% over the past year as the market reprices nuclear from a stranded asset class to a critical infrastructure necessity, driven by AI data-center power demand and clean energy mandates.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: BWXT offers unusually high contract visibility (multi-year U.S. Navy and DOE contracts with high switching costs) combined with a genuine commercial nuclear growth optionality. At $199.75 (55x trailing P/E), the valuation looks stretched relative to its industrial comps, which is the primary risk — any earnings miss or contract delay could compress the multiple sharply. The bearish view from Deutsche Bank (Hold, $180 target) centers on rich valuation and uncertainty over the pace of small modular reactor (SMR) commercialization. The bull case from BTIG ($235 target, +18% upside) rests on BWXT's technology-agnostic position across naval propulsion, DOE enrichment, and SMR components, with the organic revenue trajectory supporting 15–20% annual earnings growth through 2028. The pullback from $210 to $200 this week creates a sensible entry point for long-term thematic investors with a nuclear energy conviction.

trends.title: Oil Shock & Energy Sector Outperformance
trends.detail: Brent crude surged above $110/barrel (+55% month-to-date by March 20) as military operations in Iran and threats to the Strait of Hormuz triggered a supply-risk premium. Energy was the only S&P sector in positive territory, up ~2.8% on the week and over 32% year-to-date, with fertilizer producers (e.g., CF Industries +30% in 30 days) also benefiting from supply disruption to global nitrogen markets.

trends.title: Fed Policy Repricing: Cuts Off the Table, Hikes Possible
trends.detail: The March FOMC meeting delivered a hold at 3.50–3.75%, but the post-meeting reaction was sharply negative as markets repriced out all expected 2026 cuts. Futures now assign less than 17% probability to any cut by year-end, while hike odds have climbed to ~10–20% on prediction markets — a dramatic reversal from early February's expectation of 2–3 cuts. Higher-for-longer rates are compressing growth stock multiples, particularly in software and small-cap.

trends.title: AI Infrastructure Capex Theme Remains Intact
trends.detail: Despite market-wide weakness, the AI infrastructure spending narrative continues with strong earnings from Marvell (record Q4 revenue $2.22B, +22% YoY; FY27 guidance approaching $11B), CrowdStrike (Q4 ARR +24% YoY, $5.25B ending ARR), and SanDisk (Q2 revenue +61% YoY, NAND shortage thesis intact). Cloudflare secured its largest-ever $130M contract and surged 12% in the month, benefiting from AI agent traffic doubling.

trends.title: Storage Supercycle: WDC/SNDK Volatility
trends.detail: The storage sector saw extreme intra-week moves as Western Digital and the newly spun-off SanDisk hit all-time highs mid-week before a sharp Friday correction. WDC surged to $314.92 on March 17 on bullish investor-day guidance, only to close the week at $293. The WDC secondary offering of $3.17B in SanDisk shares created supply pressure, though both stocks remain 18–30% higher over 30 days, underpinned by structural NAND flash memory scarcity driven by AI data center demand.

trends.title: Sector Rotation from Growth to Value/Defense
trends.detail: Institutional investors are rotating out of high-multiple growth software toward value, energy, and select defense names as the discount rate rises and geopolitical risk premiums expand. Vanguard and others have flagged the 'great rotation' back to valuation discipline, and small-cap growth (Russell 2000 -8.3% in 30 days) has borne the brunt. Within software, names with clear AI revenue (DDOG, NET, PLTR) are holding up better than pure SaaS plays (TEAM, ASAN, MDB).

risks.title: Middle East Escalation / Strait of Hormuz Disruption
risks.detail: Any escalation in the Iran conflict that physically disrupts oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz — which carries ~20% of global oil — could push Brent toward $140/barrel, as modeled by ING Think. President Trump's Friday statement considering 'winding down' U.S. military presence provides a potential off-ramp, but the geopolitical situation remains highly fluid. Equity markets have already priced in considerable risk premium; further escalation would be a severe negative.

risks.title: Stagflation: Rising Inflation + Slowing Growth
risks.detail: Oil-driven inflation is colliding with a softening labor market (Powell acknowledged 'zero' net job growth) in a classic stagflation scenario. The Fed's dual-mandate is paralyzed — cutting risks inflation; hiking risks a recession. TradeStation and multiple strategists highlighted this crossroads in March 2026, noting the Fed has limited tools to address supply-side inflation. Corporate margin compression from energy input costs is a Q1 2026 earnings risk.

risks.title: Federal Reserve Rate Hike Risk
risks.detail: Markets now assign ~10–20% probability to a Fed rate hike before year-end, up from essentially zero at the start of March. The AInvest/Fortune tracker shows hike odds surpassing cut odds intra-week for the first time this cycle. A surprise hike — or even aggressive hawkish language at the April 28-29 FOMC — would reset equity valuations sharply, especially for growth stocks trading at elevated multiples.

risks.title: Gold/Risk-Off Unwind & Dollar Strength
risks.detail: Gold suffered its worst single-week decline since 2021, dropping 9.5%, suggesting a rapid de-risking and dollar-liquidity dynamic rather than a simple inflation hedge. Treasury yields climbed to 4.39% on the 10-year. If the dollar continues to strengthen (as it typically does when Fed hike odds rise), this creates additional headwinds for international tech revenues and emerging market tech (e.g., Sea Limited).

risks.title: Insider Selling & Valuation Overextension in High-Flyers
risks.detail: Several of the biggest 30-day winners are seeing aggressive insider selling: Fastly executives have sold continuously throughout a 280% 12-month rally; CF Industries insiders sold $30M+ near the top; Cloudflare CEO Prince sold $30.5M mid-week while the stock surged. Analyst consensus targets on FSLY ($13.71), TTD, and several small-cap names sit 30–50% below current prices, suggesting limited institutional conviction at current levels.

watchlist_updates.headline: NVIDIA pulls back as risk-off selling hits high-multiple AI leaders
watchlist_updates.detail: NVIDIA declined -4.2% on the week as broader market selloff and rising discount rates compressed growth valuations. The stock remains closely tied to AI capex sentiment, which continues to be supported by Marvell and CrowdStrike earnings beats, but macro headwinds dominate near-term. No material company-specific news this week; next earnings catalyst is GTC follow-through.

watchlist_updates.headline: Meta continues multi-week slide amid macro pressure and rising discount rates
watchlist_updates.detail: Meta fell -3.3% on the week in line with broad tech pressure, bringing its 30-day decline to -7.7%. The stock, which had been a top performer in early 2026, is now seeing multiple compression as Fed rate hike odds rise. No earnings catalyst until late April; AI advertising revenue momentum remains positive but macro context dominates.

watchlist_updates.headline: Microsoft sheds -3.5% on the week; Azure growth expectations intact but macro headwinds weigh
watchlist_updates.detail: Microsoft declined -3.5% as interest rate repricing drove multiple compression across enterprise software. No material company news this week. Azure AI adoption remains a key growth driver per prior commentary, and the stock's relative resilience vs. smaller-cap SaaS names reflects its quality/defensive characteristics, though it still declined alongside the broader Nasdaq.

watchlist_updates.headline: Marvell outperforms after record Q4 earnings; FY27 revenue guidance approaching $11B
watchlist_updates.detail: Marvell reported record Q4 FY2026 revenue of $2.219B (+22% YoY, +7% sequential), beating the midpoint of guidance, with data center revenue at $1.65B (+21% YoY). Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80 beat consensus of $0.71 by 13%. Management guided Q1 FY2027 revenue of $2.4B (+/-5%) and FY2027 growth of over 30% YoY, approaching $11B, driven by AI interconnect and custom silicon demand. The stock surged ~18% on the March 5 earnings date and has held up in the 30-day period despite market pressure.

watchlist_updates.headline: Arm Holdings surges +14.3% on the week, one of the few tech winners amid market selloff
watchlist_updates.detail: Arm rose sharply against the market downturn, making it a notable outlier among semiconductors. The rally appears driven by continued AI chip licensing momentum and positive analyst sentiment following prior-week strength. The stock gained from $115.75 to $132.35, a strong weekly move that signals institutional accumulation in a risk-off environment.

watchlist_updates.headline: TSMC declines amid geopolitical Taiwan risk premium and macro concerns
watchlist_updates.detail: Taiwan Semiconductor fell -2.7% on the week and -9.1% over 30 days as macro risk-off and rising geopolitical concerns (Taiwan Strait tensions secondary to Iran conflict) weighed. Advanced packaging demand from AI customers remains robust but shares are pressured by the broader macro environment and a strengthening dollar that reduces ADR appeal.

watchlist_updates.headline: CrowdStrike pulls back -7.4% on the week despite strong Q4 earnings beat earlier in the month
watchlist_updates.detail: CrowdStrike reported Q4 FY2026 earnings on March 3 with non-GAAP EPS of $1.12 (beat $0.74 estimate by 51%), Q4 net new ARR of $331M (+47% YoY), and ending ARR of $5.25B (+24% YoY). The initial post-earnings surge has since reversed as broader market pressure and rising rates hit high-multiple security names. FY2027 guidance of $5.87–5.93B in revenue was largely in-line with consensus, limiting further upside catalysts near-term.

watchlist_updates.headline: Palo Alto Networks holds up relatively well; 30-day gain of +7% on platform consolidation thesis
watchlist_updates.detail: PANW outperformed the cybersecurity group over 30 days (+7% vs. ZS -12%, CRWD -1.6%) as investors favor its platformization strategy and more stable free cash flow profile. Geopolitical tensions are broadly positive for security spending demand. No material company-specific news this week.

watchlist_updates.headline: Zscaler falls -12% over 30 days despite strong Q2 earnings beat; wider losses triggered selloff
watchlist_updates.detail: Zscaler reported FQ2 2026 revenue of $815.8M (+26% YoY, beat consensus) and non-GAAP EPS of $1.01 (beat $0.90 estimate) on Feb 26, but reported a wider GAAP net loss on elevated S&M and R&D spend in a competitive environment. Shares dropped ~9% post-earnings and have not recovered fully. ARR grew 25% to $3.4B and the company raised FY2026 guidance to $3.309–3.322B.

watchlist_updates.headline: Intuit surges +17% in 30 days after Q2 earnings beat; AI-driven growth across TurboTax, QuickBooks
watchlist_updates.detail: Intuit delivered Q2 FY2026 revenue of $4.65B (+17.4% YoY, beat by 2.75%) and non-GAAP EPS of $4.15 (beat estimate of $3.68 by 12.8%) on Feb 26. Highlights included Credit Karma revenue +23%, Consumer segment +15%, and GAAP operating income +44%. Management reiterated full-year guidance of 14–15% non-GAAP EPS growth. The stock surged from ~$390 to $455, one of the stronger tech performers in the 30-day period despite the broader market selloff.

watchlist_updates.headline: Adobe reports record Q1 FY2026 revenue of $6.4B but AI monetization uncertainty limits upside
watchlist_updates.detail: Adobe delivered Q1 FY2026 revenue of $6.40B (+12% YoY), non-GAAP EPS of $6.06 (+19% YoY), and AI-first ARR more than tripled YoY. Q2 guidance of $6.4–6.5B revenue and EPS of $5.80–5.85 exceeded consensus on EPS. However, CEO Shu Naren's announced departure after 18 years and ongoing investor debate over whether generative AI helps or disrupts Adobe's core franchise kept the stock range-bound; ADBE is down ~18% YTD heading into the week.

watchlist_updates.headline: Salesforce outperforms SaaS peers; Agentforce AI agent platform continues to gain enterprise traction
watchlist_updates.detail: Salesforce rose +1.3% on the week and +4% in 30 days, outperforming most SaaS peers. No material earnings this week; the next catalyst is Q1 FY2027 earnings. The Agentforce platform continues to generate enterprise interest and management commentary from recent industry conferences has been positive. Salesforce's defensive qualities (large installed base, recurring revenue) make it relatively attractive in a rising-rate environment.

watchlist_updates.headline: Cloudflare surges on SentinelOne AI security integration and $130M contract; CEO sells $30.5M
watchlist_updates.detail: Cloudflare gained +12.5% over 30 days, hitting new highs mid-week at $225.48 after announcing an enhanced security integration with SentinelOne and publishing its 2026 Threat Intelligence Report. The company's largest-ever contract ($130M) and doubling of AI agent traffic were cited as structural catalysts. However, CEO Matthew Prince sold $30.5M in shares (March 16–18) via a pre-established 10b5-1 plan, a watch item for investors despite the bullish analyst consensus (Baird $260, TD Cowen $265 targets).

watchlist_updates.headline: MongoDB down -28% in 30 days despite Q4 earnings beat; weak Q1 guidance drives derating
watchlist_updates.detail: MongoDB reported Q4 FY2026 revenue of $695M (+27% YoY, beat consensus), Atlas revenue +29% YoY to an annualized $2B+ run rate, and non-GAAP EPS of $1.65 vs. $0.92 estimate (79% beat). Despite the strong Q4, the stock crashed ~25% on March 3 as Q1 FY2027 guidance of $659–664M (20–21% growth) and full-year guidance of 16–18% growth disappointed investors expecting re-acceleration. The stock stabilized around $260–270 for the rest of the period.

watchlist_updates.headline: Palantir holds +11% in 30 days as government AI spending narrative supports valuation
watchlist_updates.detail: Palantir gained +11.3% over 30 days, driven by ongoing government AI/defense spending tailwinds that are arguably amplified by the Iran conflict. The stock has been one of the more resilient software names in the market downturn, reflecting its exposure to defense/intelligence spending that benefits from geopolitical stress. No material company news this week.

watchlist_updates.headline: Datadog holds flat amid market selloff; AI observability use cases support premium
watchlist_updates.detail: Datadog was roughly flat over the week (+0.4%) and slightly positive in 30 days (+2.7%), making it one of the more resilient cloud infrastructure names. The company benefited from an earlier-month surge on AI observability demand. No material news this week; Datadog's next earnings catalyst is expected in May.

watchlist_updates.headline: DigitalOcean surges +20% to 4-year highs on Oppenheimer upgrade and AI cloud pivot
watchlist_updates.detail: DigitalOcean hit a 4-year high, surging to $85.35 intra-week, driven by a strong Q4 2025 earnings report (revenue +15.5% YoY, crossing $1B annualized run-rate, net income tripling to $259M) and an Oppenheimer price target upgrade to $100 (from $85). Analysts cited DOCN's AI-optimized cloud segment growing at a 110% CAGR vs. 25% for the broader IaaS market, with datacenter capacity expansion of 209% expected by 2028.

watchlist_updates.headline: Fastly up +34% in 30 days on edge/AI momentum, but insiders selling aggressively throughout rally
watchlist_updates.detail: Fastly surged in the month on strong Q4 2025 results (revenue +22.8% YoY, $172.6M; non-GAAP operating income swung to +$21.2M from a -$2.8M loss). Management guided full-year 2026 revenue of $700–720M. However, the CTO, CFO, and CEO have sold dozens of transactions throughout the rally from $10 to $26+, and consensus analyst targets ($13.71 average) sit roughly half the current price, signaling potential valuation risk. A $7.4% pullback mid-week on note maturity/governance overhangs added caution.

watchlist_updates.headline: Snowflake declines -6% on the week; macro pressure weighs on high-multiple data cloud names
watchlist_updates.detail: Snowflake fell -6% on the week as rising rates and risk-off sentiment hit high-multiple software. No material company-specific news this week. The stock has been range-bound for 30 days as investors await more evidence of AI data cloud monetization and ongoing competition from Databricks and cloud hyperscaler native offerings.

watchlist_updates.headline: Coinbase gains +20% in 30 days as crypto markets benefit from dollar strength unwind and regulatory clarity
watchlist_updates.detail: Coinbase climbed from $164 to $197 over 30 days as cryptocurrency markets responded positively to improving US regulatory clarity and crypto's perceived role as a macro hedge. The stock surged to $208 intra-period but pulled back to $197 by week-end. No material company news this week; Coinbase earnings are next expected in May.

watchlist_updates.headline: Affirm falls -15% over 30 days as rising rate environment pressures BNPL economics
watchlist_updates.detail: Affirm declined -15.3% over 30 days as the rising rate environment — and potential rate hike expectations — pose fundamental challenges to the buy-now-pay-later model whose cost of capital increases as rates rise. The stock fell from $51.70 to $43.81. Consumer spending concerns from gas price increases add additional risk to loan origination volumes.

watchlist_updates.headline: Shift4 Payments plunges -30% over 30 days after deal collapse and earnings miss
watchlist_updates.detail: Shift4 fell sharply from $59 to $41 over the 30-day period, losing nearly a third of its value. The stock suffered a major ~15% single-day drop around February 26 (from $57 to $44), suggesting a deal-related or earnings-related catalyst. The company has faced volatility around M&A speculation and competitive dynamics in the payments space. This is a major mover warranting close attention.

watchlist_updates.headline: Block (XYZ) up +11% in 30 days after strong earnings catalyzed a sharp rally
watchlist_updates.detail: Block surged sharply around Feb 27, gaining ~20% in a single session (from $51 to $63) on strong earnings results before settling back to $59. The +10.8% 30-day gain puts it just above the major mover threshold. Cash App and Square continue to drive growth, and the company's Bitcoin-related revenue provides additional crypto market correlation.

watchlist_updates.headline: Samsara surges +23% in 30 days after Q4 earnings crush estimates with 157% EPS beat
watchlist_updates.detail: Samsara reported Q4 FY2026 on March 5 with revenue of $444.3M (+28.3% YoY, beat $426.5M estimate) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.18, crushing the $0.07 consensus by 157%. The stock surged ~12% on earnings day (March 6) from $29.58 to $35.36, and has maintained most of those gains at $33.13. FY2027 guidance was upbeat, driving significant analyst estimate upgrades. Strong fleet management and industrial IoT demand are secular tailwinds.

watchlist_updates.headline: Ciena surges +24% in 30 days and +73% in 3 months on hyperscaler AI optical networking demand
watchlist_updates.detail: Ciena reported Q1 FY2026 revenue of $1.43B (record, upper end of guidance, +33% YoY) with EPS growing 111% YoY and a record order backlog. The stock rose +13.8% this week alone (from $337 to $383), with Barclays raising its price target from $279 to $372. AI-driven demand for optical networking from cloud and service provider clients is the core thesis. Ciena's fiscal 2026 guidance of $5.9–6.3B revenue with 43.5–44.5% adjusted gross margins was well-received.

watchlist_updates.headline: SanDisk surges +18% over 30 days on NAND shortage thesis; volatile after WDC secondary offering
watchlist_updates.detail: SanDisk reported Q2 FY2026 revenue of $3.025B (+61.25% YoY, beat by 12.5%) and non-GAAP EPS of $6.20 vs. $3.54 estimate (75% beat), reinforcing the AI-driven NAND flash shortage narrative. The stock hit ~$772 intra-week before Western Digital's $3.17B secondary offering of SanDisk shares created supply pressure and pushed it back to $710. Consensus analyst target of $761 with structural bull case intact; stock is up ~1,400% since spinning off from WDC in Feb 2025.

watchlist_updates.headline: Western Digital hits all-time high $314.92 mid-week before selling off on secondary offering
watchlist_updates.detail: Western Digital surged to an all-time high of $314.92 on March 17 on bullish guidance (EPS >$20 by 2028 projected at investor conference). The rally quickly reversed as WDC executed a $3.17B secondary sale of its remaining SanDisk stake and 'sell-the-news' dynamics took hold. WDC closed the week at $293, still up +7.6% on the week but relatively flat over 30 days. WDC is transitioning to a pure-play enterprise HDD company after the SanDisk spin-off.

watchlist_updates.headline: CF Industries surges +30% in 30 days as Iran conflict disrupts global nitrogen supply
watchlist_updates.detail: CF Industries rallied from $95 to $124 over 30 days as geopolitical disruption to the Strait of Hormuz (one-third of global fertilizer supply transits the strait) created a significant premium for North American nitrogen producers. Q4 2025 revenue of $1.872B beat estimates by 7%, with gross margin expanding to 40.9%. A DOJ antitrust probe and aggressive insider selling ($30M+ from former CEO and board) are key risks. A $169.5M Orica cash settlement added near-term cash flow tailwind.

watchlist_updates.headline: Elevance Health down -16% over 30 days after CMS threatens Medicare Advantage enrollment freeze
watchlist_updates.detail: Elevance Health declined -15.8% over 30 days after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services notified the company of planned intermediate penalties that could halt new enrollments and restrict marketing for its Medicare Advantage-Prescription Drug plans starting March 31. ELV reaffirmed 2026 earnings guidance of at least $25.50 adjusted diluted EPS, but the CMS regulatory overhang has put persistent pressure on the stock since early March.

watchlist_updates.headline: Sea Limited crashes -30% in 30 days after Q4 earnings miss on rising costs and competitive pressure
watchlist_updates.detail: Sea Limited fell sharply after reporting Q4 2025 results on March 3 with net income of $410.9M — a 73% YoY increase but below the $442M analyst consensus. Total operating expenses surged 28%+ to $2.43B as Shopee invested aggressively in logistics and marketing to defend against TikTok Shop and Lazada. Shares dropped ~18–25% on the earnings day (Bloomberg: 'largest drop since 2023') and continued declining; the stock is now down ~30% from its 30-day starting level of $112.78 to $78.85.

watchlist_updates.headline: The Trade Desk down -11.8% on the week; pulls back sharply after brief earnings-driven surge
watchlist_updates.detail: The Trade Desk dropped -11.8% on the week, giving back most of the post-earnings gains from early March (it had surged from ~$24 to $29.79 on March 5 after earnings). The macro environment and digital advertising spending concerns weighed on the stock, which pulled back from $27+ to $24. This is a significant weekly move that crossed the 5% material threshold.

watchlist_updates.headline: Pinterest up +14% in 30 days on advertising revenue momentum and Amazon partnership gains
watchlist_updates.detail: Pinterest has been a quiet outperformer over 30 days, rising from $16.38 to $18.68 (+14%). The stock benefited from strong digital advertising sentiment in early March and continued momentum from its Amazon shoppable ads partnership. In a weak market, PINS has held its gains, though no major news this specific week.

watchlist_updates.headline: GitLab down -23% in 30 days on competitive concerns and growth deceleration fears
watchlist_updates.detail: GitLab has been a significant underperformer over 30 days, declining from $28.98 to $22.25 (-23.2%). The decline reflects concerns about competition from GitHub (Microsoft) in the AI-assisted DevSecOps space and potential growth deceleration. No specific earnings this week but the persistent price pressure suggests institutional distribution.

watchlist_updates.headline: Rubrik declines -7.8% on the week as cyber security names broadly sell off
watchlist_updates.detail: Rubrik pulled back -7.8% on the week, one of the larger declines in the cybersecurity group. The stock remains near its post-IPO trading range. No specific company news; the broader selloff in high-multiple cyber names (CRWD -7.4%, ZS -1.5%) weighed on RBRK as well.

watchlist_updates.headline: C3.ai down -20% in 30 days; AI hype premium continues to erode amid revenue growth concerns
watchlist_updates.detail: C3.ai has declined -20.1% over 30 days, underperforming even in a weak market. The stock fell from $10.60 to $8.47, reflecting ongoing investor skepticism about the pace of enterprise AI application adoption and profitability timeline. C3.ai's revenue growth has been decelerating and competition from cloud hyperscaler AI platforms intensifies. No material news this week.

watchlist_updates.headline: LPL Financial down -12% in 30 days as rising rate uncertainty pressures wealth management
watchlist_updates.detail: LPL Financial declined -11.8% over 30 days as rising rates create uncertainty for advisory asset valuations and potential client redemptions. The stock fell from $332 to $293. No company-specific news this week; broader financial services weakness driven by macro uncertainty is the primary driver.

watchlist_updates.headline: Atlassian down -12% in 30 days as high-multiple SaaS names face multiple compression
watchlist_updates.detail: Atlassian fell -11.8% over 30 days as rising rate expectations compressed valuations for high-multiple enterprise software. The stock declined from $83.61 to $73.73. No material company news; next earnings are in late April. Cloud transition continues but at a slower pace than originally projected.

watchlist_updates.headline: Bill.com down -11% in 30 days; SMB payment software facing macro headwinds
watchlist_updates.detail: Bill.com declined -11.3% over 30 days from $44.91 to $39.85 as SMB-focused payments software faces headwinds from a weaker economic backdrop and rising energy costs affecting small business cash flows. The stock has been rangebound around $40 after the decline.

watchlist_updates.headline: Elastic down -13% over 30 days after earnings-related selloff; partial bounce this week
watchlist_updates.detail: Elastic fell from $61 to $52 around late February (likely on earnings/guidance miss) before recovering partially to $53.31. The 30-day return of -12.6% reflects the initial selloff. A modest +3.5% this week suggests some stabilization. No material news specific to this week.

watchlist_updates.headline: Asana declines -13% over 30 days; project management SaaS faces continued headwinds
watchlist_updates.detail: Asana fell -12.6% over 30 days from $7.61 to $6.65, with this week's -5.1% decline the largest single-week drop. The stock trades at near multi-year lows. Revenue growth deceleration and losses have made investors skeptical. No material news this week beyond continued fundamental weakness concerns.

watchlist_updates.headline: Thermo Fisher down -8% in 30 days; life sciences services face modest demand headwinds
watchlist_updates.detail: Thermo Fisher declined -7.6% over 30 days from $513 to $474, with a notable drop from ~$516 to $464 around the March 6 period (possibly on macro concerns). Some recovery since then (+2.2% this week). No material company news this week. Life sciences demand remains solid but valuation re-rating continues as risk-free rate rises.
